opportunity.statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊠(⊤ : α))
opportunity.unlocks: A reusable operator-level test for GödelFixpoint existence, enabling systematic separation/countermodel construction (including targeting the high-priority non-confluence boundary in op_000005 by controlling when GödelFixpoint exists independently of HenkinFixpoint).
opportunity.why_now: The op_000004 construction idea makes clear that the failure mechanism is ‘⊠ has no fixed point’; combined with the already-derived lemma `gf_equiv_reft_top` (under `C5`) it becomes visible that any Gödel fixpoint must be equivalent to `⊠⊤`, so existence collapses to the single idempotence-at-top condition `⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊠⊤`.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not a nearby corollary of one model: it is a sharp, theory-wide characterization that replaces an existential fixed-point statement with a concrete checkable equation at `⊤`, exposing a general obstruction/criterion usable across many future constructions and boundary proofs.

opportunity.statement: In `ACR`, introduce an operator-level fixpoint type `Fixpoint (f : α → α) := {x : α // x ≐ f x}` and prove the reusable criterion: `Equivalent (f (⊤ : α)) (f (f (⊤ : α))) → Nonempty (Fixpoint f)` (witness `⟨f ⊤, h⟩`).
opportunity.unlocks: Turns future separation/model tasks (e.g. `op_000004` / `op_000005`) into the concrete goal “make `□` (or `⊠`) stabilize on `⊤` while the other does not”, and more generally provides the missing bridge from operator equations on `⊤` to explicit fixed-point constructions for arbitrary operator-words built from `□`/`⊠`.
opportunity.why_now: The solved proof of `op_000001` makes it explicit that the reverse direction is entirely the construction `g := ⊠⊤` from a top-stabilization equation; abstracting that construction reveals a general criterion independent of the specific `⊠` axioms.
opportunity.why_not_peripheral: This is not a nearby corollary about `⊠` alone: it refactors the core mechanism into a word-agnostic lemma that directly supports the agenda’s operator-level existence theory and makes separation countermodels systematically targetable via simple top-stabilization equations.
